title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski hit a walk-off single in the 10th inning, securing a 3-2 Braves win over the Red Sox.
Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early allowed two solo home runs, resulting in a 2-0 lead for the Braves.
Boston Red Sox fell to 18-26 for the season, struggling with 1-for-3 hitting with runners in scoring position.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Patriots unlikely to reunite with Stefon Diggs due to pending A.J. Brown trade affecting cap space.
Diggs, who had 1,045 yards last season, interested in returning, but Patriots focus on A.J. Brown deal.
Patriots may not afford Diggs after trading for Brown, alongside $68 million spent on Romeo Doubs.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, 15, went missing Tuesday afternoon near 965 Blue Hill Ave. in Dorchester, Boston police report.
LaGrant-Gomes is described as a Black male, 5'8", thin build, with brown eyes and black hair.
Anyone with information is urged to contact District B-3 detectives or use the Boston Police CrimeStoppers service.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, is accused of shooting his girlfriend's mother and another man in Dorchester on Mother's Day.
The incident took place at 69 Kingsdale Street, resulting in life-threatening injuries for the woman and non-life-threatening injuries for the man.
Broomes faces charges of assault with intent to murder and firearms violations; he is held without bail pending a May 21 hearing.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Former Red Sox players, including Chris Sale and Xander Bogaerts, continue to excel across Major League Baseball in 2026.
Ex-Red Sox pitcher Chris Sale has revitalized his career with a sub-3.00 ERA for the Atlanta Braves in 2026.
Kyle Schwarber leads MLB with 18 home runs in 2026, having hit multiple home runs against the Red Sox recently.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony no longer wearing wrist brace, played catch Friday
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox player Roman Anthony removed his wrist brace after a cortisone injection for a right wrist sprain.
Anthony played catch before Friday's game against the Braves, indicating progress in his recovery from the sprain.
Anthony has not yet resumed batting but hopes to start soon based on his body's response to throwing.

title: Jeep drives into Norwood day care
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Jeep accidentally drove into the BrightPath daycare in Norwood, causing moderate structural damage.
No injuries were reported; children and staff were safely evacuated after the incident.
Daycare temporarily closed as authorities assess structural damage; crash remains under investigation.

title: Here’s what experts are predicting the Patriots’ 2026 record will be
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts predict the New England Patriots will finish the 2026 NFL season with a 10-7 record.
The Patriots face a challenging 2026 schedule, including games against Seattle, Denver, and Chicago.
Only one expert, Brian Hines, predicts a more optimistic 12-5 record for the Patriots in 2026.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Marty, Life is Short,' a Martin Short documentary, is now streaming on Netflix.
'Pop Culture Jeopardy!' Season 2 with Colin Jost is available on Netflix.
'Welcome to Wrexham' Season 5 featuring Ryan Reynolds is available on Hulu.

title: Mayor Wu remains firm as councilors consider rejecting her budget
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu proposed a $4.9 billion budget, the smallest spending increase since the financial crisis.
City Council members consider rejecting Wu's budget, hoping to force a revision with increased spending.
The Council must decide on the budget by June 10; failure to act results in automatic adoption.
